summary: Tested iter92 AI portrait generator. Backend: created /app/backend/tests/test_iter92_ai_portrait.py with 9 tests — 8 PASS, 1 FAIL (batch returns only 1/3 portraits because the LLM_API_KEY budget is exhausted: 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 1.438, Max budget: 1.4'). Single-portrait endpoint works end-to-end (real Gemini Nano Banana JPEG, ~50s). Auth guard, invalid-style fallback, and PATCH /api/characters portrait_url whitelist all verified. Frontend: code-reviewed PortraitGenerator.js — every required data-testid is present and wired correctly (portrait-generator, generate-portraits-btn, portrait-gender-input, portrait-description-input, portrait-upload-label, portrait-file-input, portrait-options-grid, portrait-option-{style}, clear-portrait-btn). Could not playwright-walk all the way to Review step within 3 attempts because step 5 (Abilities → Standard Array) uses HTML5 drag-and-drop to assign scores to STR/DEX/CON/INT/WIS/CHA slots which Playwright needs explicit dispatch for; per Loop Prevention I stopped after 4 builder walkthroughs. Home regression (character-sort + campaign-sort) confirmed visible on /home.

minor.issue: Only 1 of 3 parallel calls succeeds in this preview env because the LLM_API_KEY budget cap (1.4) is already exceeded — the second & third calls return ChatError(BadRequestError: Budget has been exceeded). Code is correct and gracefully degrades (returns each style with an 'error' field), but iter92 acceptance criterion '>=2 of 3 should have non-empty image_base64' fails because of an environmental quota limit, not the implementation. Increase the LLM_API_KEY budget or expect partial results.

action_items: Bump the LLM_API_KEY monthly budget — Gemini image-preview is at 1.438 / 1.4 max, blocking parallel batch generation. The single-portrait endpoint still squeezes through but the 3-image batch loses 2/3 to budget-exceeded errors. This is the only thing keeping the new feature from working at 100%.
(Optional) Make the batch endpoint serial-with-retry or fall back to fewer styles when the budget is tight, so a single budget-exceeded error doesn't kill 2/3 portraits.
critical_code_review_comments: ai_portrait.py is clean: pydantic input validation, style whitelist with photoreal fallback, asyncio.gather for batch, per-style try/except so one failure doesn't sink the whole batch — solid pattern.

PortraitGenerator.js correctly uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, has loading/disabled state, success+error toasts, file-size guard (4MB), MIME guard, and clear-portrait button. All interactive elements have data-testid.
Minor: LlmChat session_id is fresh per request — fine for one-shot image gen but won't reuse the model session. Not a bug, just noting.

Minor: ai_portrait.py imports `LLM_KEY` only inside the try/except for configured AI provider — if the package is present but the env var is missing at import time, LLM_KEY is None and the endpoint correctly returns 503. Good.
characters.py PATCH whitelist correctly persists portrait_url (verified via PATCH→GET round-trip).

success_rate.backend: 89% (8/9 — the only failure is the batch test failing because the LLM budget is already exhausted in this env, not a code defect)
success_rate.frontend: code-reviewed 100% (all required data-testids present, PortraitGenerator wiring + upload path + clear button all correct); browser walkthrough only reached Step 5/8 due to Standard Array drag-and-drop UX — not a regression

seed_data_creation: Test creates throw-away characters prefixed TEST_iter92_portrait_<hex> and deletes them in fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter92 backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_iter92_ai_portrait.py. The batch test will keep failing until the LLM_API_KEY budget is raised — this is environmental. Single-portrait endpoint works (verified ~50s for real Gemini JPEG, ~1.1MB). PATCH /api/characters/{id} now accepts portrait_url (whitelist fix confirmed via PATCH→GET round-trip). Frontend builder walkthrough is gated on Step 5 Abilities drag-and-drop — to test the PortraitGenerator UI directly, either (a) seed a complete characterDraft via localStorage before mounting, (b) use Playwright's dispatchEvent('dragstart'/'drop') to assign the 6 Standard Array values, or (c) bypass to /characters/{id}/edit on a saved character. All required data-testids on PortraitGenerator are present and verified by code review.
rca of the issue: Batch test failure RCA: backend logs show `litellm.BadRequestError: OpenAIException - Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 1.4383, Max budget: 1.4` for the 2nd and 3rd parallel calls inside generate_portrait_batch. The LLM_API_KEY/litellm proxy enforces a hard 1.4 USD cap on gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview and one image already pushes the running total over. Mitigation: raise the budget cap, or have the frontend/backend serialize the 3 calls + add retry-with-backoff; alternatively reduce to 2 styles. The code path itself is correct — it returns a 200 with `portraits: [{style, error: 'ChatError', ...}, ...]` so the UI can show 'Generation failed' tiles cleanly.
